title: 'Shogun' actress Anna Sawai wins Golden Globe 44 years after Yoko Shimada won for same role
text: Anna Sawai won the Golden Globe for best actress in a drama series for her role in "Shōgun" at the 2025 Golden Globes. The win was historic as Sawai became the first Asian actress to win a Golden Globe for this category. The late Yoko Shimada won the same category for the same role in the 1980 iteration of "Shōgun," creating a 44-year gap between Asian actresses winning in this category for the same role. Sawai beat out Kathy Bates ("Matlock"), Emma D'Arcy ("House of the Dragon"), Maya Erskine ("Mr. & Mrs. Smith"), Keira Knightley ("Black Doves"), and Keri Russell ("The Diplomat"). In September 2024, she became the first Asian actress to win an Emmy for best actress in a drama series. Three Japanese actors secured major awards for "Shōgun" in a single night at the 2025 Golden Globes, and "Shōgun" also won the Globe for best drama series, marking a historic sweep.
